name: Scribbble
subtitle: Draw on anything, instantly
featuredHeadline: Turn your screen into a canvas.
blurb: A beautiful screen-annotation app for educators, content creators and presenters. Draw on anything, anywhere, instantly.
description:
Scribbble lets you draw directly on top of your screen — over any app, any window, any moment. Perfect for teaching, demos, presentations and recordings where pointing isn’t enough.

Grab a pen, circle the thing that matters, sketch an arrow, highlight a flow — then clear it all with a keystroke. Smooth, pressure-aware strokes and a thoughtfully minimal toolbar keep the focus on what you’re explaining, not on the tool.

highlights.title: Draw on anything
highlights.text: Annotate over any app or window in real time.

highlights.title: Made for presenters
highlights.text: Built for educators, creators and live demos.

highlights.title: Clear in a keystroke
highlights.text: Wipe the canvas instantly and keep flowing.

whatsNew:
• Redesigned floating toolbar
• New highlighter & shape tools
• Performance improvements for 4K displays

reviews.title: A must for teaching
reviews.author: msdraws
reviews.text: I teach online and Scribbble has completely changed how I explain things. Circling and underlining live is magic.

reviews.title: So clean
reviews.author: demodave
reviews.text: The toolbar gets out of the way and the strokes feel great. Exactly what I wanted.

name: NameMySpace
subtitle: Name & color your macOS Spaces
blurb: No more “Desktop 1, 2, 3…”. Give every macOS Space a real name and a color, then jump straight to it with a keystroke.
description:
NameMySpace lives quietly in your menu bar and finally fixes the one thing macOS never did — it lets you give your desktops real names and colors instead of forgettable numbers.

Set up a Space for Design, one for Email, one for Deep Work, and switch between them instantly with a keyboard shortcut. Color-coding makes the active Space obvious at a glance, so you always know exactly where you are.

Lightweight, native, and built to disappear into your workflow.

highlights.title: Custom names
highlights.text: Replace “Desktop 1, 2, 3” with names that mean something.

highlights.title: Color-coded
highlights.text: A glance tells you which Space you’re on.

highlights.title: Keyboard shortcuts
highlights.text: Switch to any named Space instantly.

highlights.title: Menu bar app
highlights.text: Always there, never in the way.

whatsNew:
• Smoother Space-switching animations
• New color palette presets
• Fixes for multi-display setups

reviews.title: Finally!
reviews.author: spaces_lover
reviews.text: How was this not built into macOS already. I have 6 desktops and now I actually know which is which.

reviews.title: Tiny but mighty
reviews.author: deepwork
reviews.text: Lives in the menu bar, never gets in the way, switches instantly. Perfect.

name: TimeSpan
subtitle: A precision time tracker for your menu bar
blurb: A precision time tracker that lives in your menu bar. Start a timer, track projects, and see exactly where your hours go — all without leaving your workflow.
description:
TimeSpan is a beautifully native macOS time tracker that lives in your menu bar. Start a timer with one click, tag it to a project, and get back to work — no context switching, no clutter.

When you want the picture, animated donut charts break down your month at a glance so you can see exactly where your hours went across every project. An optional frosted-glass floating timer keeps the running clock visible wherever you are, and smart fuzzy search resurfaces recent projects so you can resume in a single click.

Lightweight, private, and entirely local — your data never leaves your Mac.

highlights.title: Menu bar native
highlights.text: One click to start tracking. Always visible, never intrusive.

highlights.title: Project insights
highlights.text: Animated donut charts break down your month at a glance.

highlights.title: Floating timer
highlights.text: A draggable frosted-glass window keeps time always in sight.

highlights.title: Smart search
highlights.text: Fuzzy matching finds projects instantly; recent ones surface first.

whatsNew:
• Initial release
• Menu bar timer with project tracking
• Monthly analytics with donut charts
• Floating timer & smart search

reviews.title: Exactly what I needed
reviews.author: billablehrs
reviews.text: Lives in my menu bar, one click to start, and the monthly charts are gorgeous. Replaced three other apps with this.

reviews.title: Beautiful and private
reviews.author: focusmode
reviews.text: Love that the data stays local. The floating timer is a brilliant touch for staying honest about my hours.

name: Home127
subtitle: All your local servers, one click away
blurb: Home127 discovers the web servers running on your machine and drops them right into your menu bar for instant access.
description:
If you’re a developer, you’ve got servers running on ports you can never remember. Home127 finds every local web server running on your Mac and lists them in your menu bar, ready to open in a click.

No more typing localhost:3000, :8080, :5173 from memory. Home127 keeps a live, always-current list of what’s actually running — so you spend less time hunting for ports and more time building.

highlights.title: Auto-discovery
highlights.text: Finds every running local server automatically.

highlights.title: Menu bar native
highlights.text: One click to open any localhost in your browser.

highlights.title: Always current
highlights.text: The list updates live as servers start and stop.

highlights.title: Stays out of the way
highlights.text: Quiet, light, and built for focus.

whatsNew:
• Initial public release
• Live server discovery
• Menu bar quick-open

reviews.title: Saves me every day
reviews.author: portjuggler
reviews.text: I run a dozen projects at once. This is the first thing I open in the morning now.

reviews.title: Just works
reviews.author: fullstackfran
reviews.text: Does exactly one thing and does it well. Wish I’d had this years ago.
